question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TracingMiddleware wraps every single HTTP handler with no exclusion, causing every request (including health checks) to allocate a tracingResponseWriter, and under high concurrency exhaust memory?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getClientIpAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with an X-Real-IP header containing thousands of comma-separated IP addresses (a common proxy chain format), causing the entire string to be stored and logged without truncation, exhausting log disk space?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with Unicode characters in the URL path that are preserved by request.URL.RequestURI(), causing fmt.Sprintf to allocate multi-byte strings per request and amplify memory usage compared to ASCII paths?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: Write()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where the inner handler writes a large response body, causing tracingResponseWriter.Write() to store the full body in w.data while also forwarding it to the underlying ResponseWriter, effectively doubling memory usage per response?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with paths containing special log format characters (e.g., logrus format tokens), causing log.Info(message) to misinterpret the message and produce malformed log entries that corrupt the log file and crash log consumers?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with a path that is a very long string of repeated characters, causing internalPaths[path] to perform a string hash computation on a large key for every request, exhausting CPU under high concurrency?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getClientIpAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user set the X-Forwarded-For header to a value containing special characters (e.g., tab, carriage return), causing log injection that splits a single log entry into multiple lines, inflating log file size and exhausting disk space?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with an extremely long HTTP method string, which is read via request.Method and embedded in the log message by fmt.Sprintf without any length check, causing log entries of unbounded size and disk exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: newTracingResponseWriter()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where tracingResponseWriter objects are not promptly garbage collected due to long-lived request goroutines, causing heap memory to grow unboundedly and crash the process?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with paths that are just below the internalPaths match threshold (e.g., /health/liveness/ with trailing slash), causing them to be logged at Info level instead of Debug, amplifying log volume and exhausting disk?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getClientIpAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with an X-Real-IP header containing a very long IPv6 address string, which passes the len(ipAddress) == 0 check and is stored without validation, causing unbounded string allocation in the log message?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of any maximum log message size enforcement in TracingMiddleware to write arbitrarily large log entries via log.Info(message), causing the log file to grow without bound and exhaust disk space?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: Write()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the inner handler to call tracingResponseWriter.Write() with a slice backed by a very large underlying array, causing the w.data assignment to retain a reference to the large array and prevent GC from reclaiming it, exhausting heap memory?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with paths containing null bytes that are preserved by request.URL.RequestURI(), causing log.Info(message) to write null bytes into the log file, corrupting it and crashing log consumers that cannot handle null bytes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with paths that are valid but extremely long (e.g., 64KB), causing the string comparison in internalPaths[path] to allocate a large map key and perform a slow hash, degrading throughput and causing a CPU-based DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getClientIpAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with both X-Real-IP and X-Forwarded-For headers absent and a malformed RemoteAddr that causes net.SplitHostPort to return an error, resulting in an empty IP string being logged and masking the true source of a DoS attack?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with paths containing ANSI escape codes, causing log.Info(message) to write terminal control sequences into the log file, corrupting log viewers and potentially crashing log monitoring tools?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send a large number of concurrent requests, each causing TracingMiddleware to call inner.ServeHTTP(tracingResponseWriter, request) and hold a tracingResponseWriter in memory for the duration of the request, exhausting heap memory under sustained concurrency?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getClientIpAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user set the X-Forwarded-For header to a value of exactly 1 byte (passing the len check) but containing a special character, causing log injection that corrupts the log file structure and crashes log consumers?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with paths that contain repeated path segments (e.g., /a/a/a/.../a with thousands of segments), causing request.URL.RequestURI() to return a very long string that is then allocated in fmt.Sprintf, exhausting memory?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: Write()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the inner handler to produce a response that calls Write() with a slice that shares memory with a large buffer, causing tracingResponseWriter.data to hold a reference to the large buffer and prevent GC, leading to memory exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with paths that are valid Rosetta API paths but with extremely long query parameters, causing request.URL.RequestURI() to include the full query string in the log message, exhausting disk space?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that fmt.Sprintf allocates a new string for every request with no pooling or reuse, causing high GC pressure under sustained load that degrades throughput and causes a non-network-based DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getClientIpAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with an X-Real-IP header containing a string of 1MB or more, which passes the len(ipAddress) == 0 check and is embedded in the log message string, causing each log entry to be 1MB+ and rapidly exhausting disk space?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with paths that are not in internalPaths and contain attacker-controlled content, causing log.Info(message) to write attacker-controlled data to the log file, and by sending enough requests exhaust disk space and crash the node?
